constructor.details: Ensures the logic contract cannot be hijacked before the `initializer` runs - Sets version to `type(uint64).max` + `emit Initialized(version)` to prevent future initialization - `initialize` is where the business logic is initialized on proxies - For more info see comments in 'Initializable.sol'

getAllocFixByKey(uint256,bytes32,uint256.details: Get InstAllocFix fields (for a contract client)

getAllocFixesLenByKey(uint256,bytes32,ui.details: Get count of InstAllocFix items (for a contract client)

getCallResByReqId(bytes16).details: Get a result for a prior write function call invoked from off-chain by this caller - Subject to storage lag when retrieved off-chain shortly after write, prefer the `ReqAck` event in that case

params.reqId: Request ID of a prior call

getCallResBySeqNum(uint40).details: Get a result for a prior write function call invoked from off-chain by this caller - Subject to storage lag when retrieved off-chain shortly after write, prefer the `ReqAck` event in that case

params.seqNum: Sequence number of a prior call by this caller

getInstRev(uint256,uint256).details: Get instrument revenue, empty if not found

params.pid: Proposal ID, >0 to query a proposal, 0 to query executed state

getInstRevForInstDate(uint256,string,uin.details: Get instrument revenue, empty if not found

params.pid: Proposal ID, >0 to query a proposal, 0 to query executed state

getInstRevs(uint256,string,uint256,uint2.details: Get instrument revenues - Scope of the results is conditioned on the filter of params: `pid`, `instName`, `earnDate` - Caller must page outputs to avoid gas issues, see PAGE_REQUESTS

params.count: Items to get, 0 = [iBegin:] (may exceed gas), May call `getInstRevsLen` with same inputs
params.earnDate: An earn date to filter or 0 to get all
params.iBegin: Index in the array to begin processing
params.instName: An instrument name to filter or empty to get all
params.pid: Proposal ID, >0 to query a proposal, 0 to query executed state

getInstRevsLen(uint256,string,uint256).details: Get count of instrument's revenue - Scope of the count is conditioned on the filter of params

params.earnDate: Earn date to filter or 0 to get all
params.instName: Instrument name to filter or empty to get all
params.pid: Proposal ID, >0 to query a proposal, =0 to query executed state
Proposal ID, identifies an existing proposal

getSeqNum(address).details: Get next expected seq num for the given account address

returns.seqNumEx: Next expected seq num to be passed to a write function call from `account`

initialize(address,bytes16).details: Basically replaces the constructor in a proxy oriented contract - `initializer` modifier ensures this function can only be called once during deploy - See UUPS_UPGRADE_SEQ for details on how to upgrade this contract

params.creator: Creator's address for access control during setup
params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers

preUpgrade(uint40,bytes16,bytes16).details: Stage values to use in the next call to `__authorizeUpgrade`

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

propAddInstRev(uint40,bytes16,(uint256,u.details: Add instrument revenues to a proposal - CallRes: Indicates progress where `rc` is set from `AddInstRc`

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

propAddInstRevAdj(uint40,bytes16,(uint25.details: Add instrument revenue changes/fixes to a proposal - allows revenue per owner to be adjusted - Each fix will apply a relative adjustment to an owner's balance (+ or -) - Funds are optionally required from either the dropAddr or vault - Owner qty is not relevant here as it's handled separately via a RevMgr correction proposal - CallRes: Indicates progress where `rc` is set from `AddInstRc`

params.req: See struct definition - reduces stack pressure
params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

propCreate(uint256,bytes16,address,bool).details: Add an instrument revenue proposal, this creates a stub that must be populated by additional calls - Inputs validated upstream - No `_isReqReplay` since this call cannot be executed directly from off-chain

params.correction: Whether the proposal is a correction for previously incorrect values

propExecInstRev(uint256,uint256).details: Add an instrument to the executed state - No `_isReqReplay` since this call cannot be executed directly from off-chain

params.iInstRev: Index of the InstRev in the proposal, see ``getInstRevsLen(pid, '', 0)` for an upper bound

propExecuted(uint256).details: Mark proposal as executed - No `_isReqReplay` since this call cannot be executed directly from off-chain

propFinalize(uint256).details: Validate proposal is ready for voting

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC-1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

pruneProp(uint256,string,uint256).details: Remove an instrument earn date (InstRev) from a sealed proposal - This is an escape hatch to help ensure progress in `propExecute` - Size: ~2.6 KB - No `_isReqReplay` since this call cannot be executed directly from off-chain

returns.rc: Indicates progress: - Done     : Proposal is complete - NoProp   : No proposal found by pid - PropStat : Proposal is not fully uploaded, create a new proposal - NoInst   : Not found in proposal by key - LastInst : Pruning the last instrument would invalidate the proposal

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

validateInstRev(uint256,(string,bytes32,.details: Validate instrument revenue with range and integrity checks - May help preview/debug an InstRev before adding to a proposal

params.correction: Whether this relates to a correction
params.pid: Proposal ID, >0 to query related to a proposal, =0 related to executed state

getPropHdr(uint256).notice: Get instrument revenue proposal header info. - To get `InstRev` in the prop, see `getInstRevs` - To get `OwnInfo` in the prop, see `getOwnInfo`
